chapter_summary: Mary Magdalene finds the empty tomb on Sunday morning and tells Peter and John, who run to see for themselves. Mary stays weeping outside the tomb, is met by angels, and then by Jesus Himself — who calls her by name and she recognises Him. That evening Jesus appears to the disciples behind locked doors, shows them His wounds, and breathes the Holy Spirit on them. Thomas, who was absent, refuses to believe until a week later Jesus appears again and invites him to touch the wounds. Thomas cries 'My Lord and my God!' John closes with his purpose statement: these signs were written so that you may believe Jesus is the Christ and have life in His name.
main_lesson: Jesus is alive and He knows your name — and when you believe in Him, you have life that never ends.

memory_verse.kjv: Jesus saith unto him, Thomas, because thou hast seen me, thou hast believed: blessed are they that have not seen, and yet have believed.
memory_verse.little_bible: Jesus said to Thomas: 'You believed because you saw Me. But how blessed are the people who believe in Me without seeing Me — and still trust Me completely!'

parent_guide: John 20 is the resurrection chapter of John's Gospel and one of the richest in the New Testament. The empty tomb scene (vv1-10) gives us the beloved disciple believing on sight and Peter's wondering silence. Mary Magdalene's encounter (vv11-18) is one of the most intimate moments in Scripture — Jesus calling her by name, the personal tenderness of a Saviour who knows each sheep by name. The upper room appearance (vv19-23) emphasises peace, mission, and the gift of the Spirit. The Thomas narrative (vv24-29) requires pastoral care — Thomas is not a villain but a grieving man who set conditions on faith; Jesus met him where he was and gave him what he needed. The final verses (30-31) are John's explicit purpose statement — these things were written for faith. For children: emphasise that Jesus knows your name (Mary), that He is not scared of your doubts (Thomas), and that the Bible was written so you can believe and have life.
application_for_children: Jesus knows your name. He is not far away — He comes to you right where you are. When you feel sad, confused, or like you just can't believe, remember: He came to Mary in the garden. He came to Thomas in his doubt. He will come to you too. And when you believe in Him, you have life that never runs out.

verses.kjv: The first day of the week cometh Mary Magdalene early, when it was yet dark, unto the sepulchre, and seeth the stone taken away from the sepulchre.
verses.little_bible: Very early on Sunday morning, while it was still dark, Mary Magdalene came to the tomb where Jesus had been buried. She saw right away that the big stone had been moved away from the entrance.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Very early Sunday, Mary Magdalene went to the tomb. The stone was moved away!
verses.meaning: Mary came in the dark, led by love and grief. She was the first to arrive and the first to discover the empty tomb. Love always shows up — even in the dark.
verses.memory_phrase: Love shows up even in the dark.
verses.prayer: Lord, help me come to You even in my dark and sad times — like Mary did. You are always there waiting.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think Mary came to the tomb so early, even when it was still dark?
verses.family_discussion: Mary came out of love and grief. How does love keep us coming back to Jesus even when things are hard?
verses.do_it_today: Come to Jesus first today — before the busyness begins. Start your day with Him, like Mary did.
verses.illustration_prompt: A woman in a dark blue robe walking alone along a garden path in the purple pre-dawn darkness, a round stone visible ahead rolled back from a dark tomb entrance. Soft starlight and the first blush of dawn on the horizon. Storybook style.

verses.kjv: Then she runneth, and cometh to Simon Peter, and to the other disciple, whom Jesus loved, and saith unto them, They have taken away the Lord out of the sepulchre, and we know not where they have laid him.
verses.little_bible: Mary ran as fast as she could to find Simon Peter and the disciple Jesus loved, and she told them breathlessly: 'They have taken the Lord out of the tomb! We don't know where they have put Him!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Mary ran to Peter and John! She said, 'Someone has taken Jesus away from the tomb!'
verses.meaning: Mary's first instinct was that someone had stolen the body. She didn't imagine resurrection — she imagined loss. When we don't know what God is doing, we jump to the worst conclusion. But God had something better.
verses.memory_phrase: When you don't understand, run to your friends — and to Jesus.
verses.prayer: God, when I can't understand what is happening, help me run toward the people who can help me, and toward You.
verses.discussion_question: Has something ever confused you so much that you ran to tell someone? What happened?
verses.family_discussion: Mary thought the worst. How do we help each other not jump to the worst conclusion when something confusing happens?
verses.do_it_today: If something is worrying you today, tell it to a trusted person and to God. Don't carry it alone.
verses.illustration_prompt: Mary running down a narrow path in early morning light, hair loose, robe flying, face full of alarm, toward two figures in the distance standing in a doorway. Storybook style.

verses.kjv: Peter therefore went forth, and that other disciple, and came to the sepulchre.
verses.little_bible: Peter and the other disciple — the one Jesus loved — immediately set off together toward the tomb.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Peter and the other disciple set off running toward the tomb.
verses.meaning: They didn't debate whether to go — they went. When we hear something about Jesus, the right response is to go and find out for yourself.
verses.memory_phrase: When you hear about Jesus, go and see.
verses.prayer: Lord, give me a heart that chases after You. Help me always run toward You, not away.
verses.discussion_question: When you hear something exciting, do you want to go and see for yourself or wait and find out later?
verses.family_discussion: Peter and John both ran toward the tomb — toward the mystery, toward Jesus. How can your family cultivate that instinct: running toward Jesus, not away?
verses.do_it_today: Take one step toward Jesus today — open the Bible, say a prayer, go to church. Just go.
verses.illustration_prompt: Two men running together down a road in morning light, one slightly ahead of the other, both with purpose on their faces, the path ahead leading toward a garden. Storybook style.

verses.kjv: So they ran both together: and the other disciple did outrun Peter, and came first to the sepulchre.
verses.little_bible: They both ran, but the disciple Jesus loved was faster — he reached the tomb first.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: They both ran! The other disciple was faster and got there first.
verses.meaning: This small detail — one outrunning the other — is the memory of an eyewitness. John remembered exactly how it happened. Real witnesses tell real details.
verses.memory_phrase: God's Word is full of real, true details.
verses.prayer: Lord, thank You that the Bible tells real stories from real people. Help me trust it completely.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think John remembered who ran faster? What does that tell us about this story?
verses.family_discussion: Real eyewitness memories include small details like who ran faster. How does this kind of detail help us trust that the resurrection really happened?
verses.do_it_today: Thank God today that the people who wrote the New Testament were real people who really saw these things.
verses.illustration_prompt: Two men running on a garden path, one clearly in the lead by a few paces, both breathing hard, the tomb entrance visible ahead in the morning light. Storybook style.

verses.kjv: And he stooping down, and looking in, saw the linen clothes lying; yet went he not in.
verses.little_bible: He bent down and peered inside the tomb — and saw the linen burial cloths just lying there. But he didn't go inside. He stopped at the door.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: He bent down and looked in. He saw the burial cloths — but did not go in.
verses.meaning: Arriving first but pausing at the door — something held him back. Wonder can stop us in our tracks. Some moments are too sacred to rush.
verses.memory_phrase: Some moments call us to stop and wonder.
verses.prayer: Lord, give me holy wonder — a heart that pauses before sacred things rather than rushing past them.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever stopped before doing something because it felt too important to rush? What was it?
verses.family_discussion: John paused at the threshold. Is there room in your family's life for pausing and wondering, not just rushing on?
verses.do_it_today: Pause before you open the Bible next time. Take a breath and ask God to make the moment sacred.
verses.illustration_prompt: A young man bent low, peering into a dark tomb entrance, morning light illuminating the inside where folded white burial cloths lie on a stone ledge. His face is a mixture of wonder and hesitation. Storybook style.

verses.kjv: Then cometh Simon Peter following him, and went into the sepulchre, and seeth the linen clothes lie,
verses.little_bible: Then Peter arrived — and Peter being Peter, he went straight inside the tomb. He looked and saw the linen burial cloths lying there.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Peter arrived and went straight inside. He saw the burial cloths lying there.
verses.meaning: Peter, bold and impulsive, went right in. Sometimes we need the person who will just go — who acts while others wonder.
verses.memory_phrase: God uses the bold and the wondering.
verses.prayer: Lord, make me bold enough to step in when You are calling me forward, and wise enough to wonder when I should.
verses.discussion_question: Would you be more like John — pausing at the door — or more like Peter — going straight in? Which one do you think you are?
verses.family_discussion: God uses all personality types. Peter went in boldly; John held back. How does your family reflect different ways of approaching faith and wonder?
verses.do_it_today: If there is something you have been hesitating to step into, ask: 'Is this the moment God wants me to go in?'
verses.illustration_prompt: Peter stepping boldly through the low tomb entrance, one hand on the stone doorway, the other reaching forward, his expression alert and wide-eyed. Burial cloths visible inside. Morning light from behind. Storybook style.

verses.kjv: And the napkin, that was about his head, not lying with the linen clothes, but wrapped together in a place by itself.
verses.little_bible: He also noticed the cloth that had covered Jesus' head — it was not scattered with the linen cloths but was folded up neatly, lying separately in its own place.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The cloth from Jesus' head was neatly folded and set apart — all by itself.
verses.meaning: A folded cloth is not the work of grave robbers. This is one of the most quietly powerful details in the resurrection account. Someone had left deliberately — and left things tidy.
verses.memory_phrase: The folded cloth says: Jesus left on purpose.
verses.prayer: Lord, thank You that the resurrection was not chaos — it was Your plan, perfectly completed. I can trust a God who is that intentional.
verses.discussion_question: Why would the folded cloth be an important clue that someone had not stolen the body?
verses.family_discussion: Sometimes God puts small clues in the details for people who are looking carefully. What small details has God put in your life that point to His presence?
verses.do_it_today: Pay attention to small details today. God often speaks in the things we normally rush past.
verses.illustration_prompt: Close-up inside a tomb: on one side, linen cloths lying flat; on the other side, a neatly folded face cloth sitting alone on the stone ledge. Soft light. The detail speaks for itself. Storybook style.

verses.kjv: Then went in also that other disciple, which came first to the sepulchre, and he saw, and believed.
verses.little_bible: Then John — the disciple who had arrived first — finally went in too. He looked at everything, and he believed.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Then John went in, looked at everything, and he believed!
verses.meaning: Seeing the evidence — and believing. John was the first to believe in the resurrection on the basis of what he saw. Evidence leads to faith for those with eyes to see.
verses.memory_phrase: Look at the evidence — then believe.
verses.prayer: Lord, as I look at all the evidence of the resurrection, let faith grow in my heart the way it did in John's.
verses.discussion_question: What evidence did John see that helped him believe?
verses.family_discussion: John believed before he fully understood — the next verse tells us they didn't yet understand Scripture. Faith sometimes comes before full understanding. Is that okay?
verses.do_it_today: Think of one piece of evidence for the resurrection that you find convincing. Share it with someone today.
verses.illustration_prompt: John standing inside the bright tomb, face lit with sudden conviction and wonder, looking at the folded cloths, one hand pressed to his chest as belief arrives. Morning light flooding in. Storybook style.

verses.kjv: For as yet they knew not the scripture, that he must rise again from the dead.
verses.little_bible: They still didn't understand that the Scripture had said Jesus would rise from the dead — that understanding would come later. But they believed what they saw.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: They did not yet understand the Scripture about rising from the dead — but they believed!
verses.meaning: Faith can outpace understanding. John believed before he understood all the Scripture. God welcomes faith that is still growing and still learning.
verses.memory_phrase: You can believe before you fully understand.
verses.prayer: God, I don't understand everything — but I believe. Help my understanding grow as I walk with You.
verses.discussion_question: Is it okay to believe something even when you don't fully understand it yet?
verses.family_discussion: Faith and understanding grow together — neither is complete without the other. How does your family help each other grow in both?
verses.do_it_today: Write down one thing about God you believe but don't fully understand yet. Keep it as a prayer — ask God to help you understand.
verses.illustration_prompt: Two men standing in a garden outside the tomb, looking thoughtful and awed, the early morning light around them, one with a hand on his chin thinking, the other with closed eyes and a peaceful expression. Storybook style.

verses.kjv: Then the disciples went away again unto their own home.
verses.little_bible: Then the two disciples went back home. They had seen what there was to see — and they went to think about what it all meant.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Then the two disciples walked home to think about everything.
verses.meaning: Sometimes we need to go home and sit with what we have seen or heard. Faith needs space to settle. Not every moment of wonder requires an immediate action — sometimes it just needs to be pondered.
verses.memory_phrase: Take time to think about what God has shown you.
verses.prayer: Lord, give me quiet moments to think about what You are showing me. Help me not rush past the things You are teaching me.
verses.discussion_question: When something amazing happens, do you like to talk about it right away or sit quietly and think about it first?
verses.family_discussion: The disciples went home to ponder. Does your family have space in the week for quiet reflection on what God is doing?
verses.do_it_today: Spend five minutes today in complete quiet, thinking about one thing God has done or shown you.
verses.illustration_prompt: Two figures walking slowly away from a garden toward a city in the morning light, heads bowed in thought, the world quiet around them. Storybook style.

verses.kjv: But Mary stood without at the sepulchre weeping: and as she wept, she stooped down, and looked into the sepulchre,
verses.little_bible: But Mary stayed outside the tomb, crying. Through her tears, she bent down and looked inside the tomb one more time.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Mary stayed at the tomb, crying. She bent down and looked inside again.
verses.meaning: Mary didn't leave. Love kept her there, even in grief, even when there was seemingly nothing left to see. And because she stayed, she received what Peter and John missed — the encounter with Jesus Himself.
verses.memory_phrase: Stay close to Jesus — even in tears.
verses.prayer: Lord, when I am sad and can't understand, help me stay near You like Mary did. Don't let my grief drive me away from You.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think Mary stayed while Peter and John went home?
verses.family_discussion: Love kept Mary at the tomb even in grief. How can we stay close to Jesus in difficult and painful seasons of life?
verses.do_it_today: If you are going through something sad, don't run from Jesus — stay near. Tell Him how you feel right now.
verses.illustration_prompt: Mary kneeling outside the tomb entrance, tears on her face, looking into the darkness inside, her robe pooled around her on the ground. Morning light behind her. Tender and heartbreaking. Storybook style.

verses.kjv: And seeth two angels in white sitting, the one at the head, and the other at the feet, where the body of Jesus had lain.
verses.little_bible: She looked in — and saw two angels dressed in white, sitting where Jesus' body had been. One was at the head and one was at the feet.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Inside the tomb she saw two angels in white! One at the head, one at the feet where Jesus had been.
verses.meaning: Two angels marking the place where the body had been — like bookends of a miracle. The tomb is now a testimony, not a tragedy.
verses.memory_phrase: Where Jesus was — angels now shine.
verses.prayer: God, thank You that the place of death became the place of wonder. Turn the sad places in my life into places where I see You.
verses.discussion_question: What do you think the two angels were doing sitting where Jesus had been?
verses.family_discussion: The emptiness where Jesus lay became a sacred, angel-lit space. How can the empty, sad places in our lives become places where we see God?
verses.do_it_today: Think of one hard thing in your life. Ask God to make that place a place where you see His glory.
verses.illustration_prompt: Inside the tomb: two luminous figures in white robes seated at either end of the stone ledge where Jesus had lain, a warm, soft glow around them. Mary's face appearing at the entrance looking in with red, tear-filled eyes. Storybook style.

verses.kjv: And they say unto her, Woman, why weepest thou? She saith unto them, Because they have taken away my Lord, and I know not where they have laid him.
verses.little_bible: The angels asked her: 'Woman, why are you crying?' She answered: 'Because they have taken away my Lord, and I don't know where they have put Him.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The angels asked, 'Why are you crying?' Mary said, 'They have taken my Lord and I don't know where He is!'
verses.meaning: Mary calls Jesus 'my Lord.' Even in grief, her relationship with Him is personal and possessive — He is hers. And even when she doesn't know where He is, she won't stop looking.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus is my Lord — even when I can't find Him.
verses.prayer: Jesus, You are MY Lord. Even when I can't feel You or find You, You are still mine and I am Yours.
verses.discussion_question: Notice Mary said 'my Lord' — not 'the Lord.' What does using the word 'my' tell us about how she felt about Jesus?
verses.family_discussion: A personal faith is the most powerful faith. Is Jesus 'the Lord' to your family — or 'my Lord' and 'our Lord'? What is the difference?
verses.do_it_today: Pray out loud today and say 'MY Lord' — make it personal. He is yours.
verses.illustration_prompt: Mary's face at the tomb entrance, speaking to two white-robed figures within, her expression full of grief and loss, tears streaming, hands pressed together. Soft angel light from within the tomb. Storybook style.

verses.kjv: And when she had thus said, she turned herself back, and saw Jesus standing, and knew not that it was Jesus.
verses.little_bible: After she said this, she turned around — and there was Jesus, standing right behind her. But she didn't recognise Him. She didn't know it was Jesus.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: She turned around — and Jesus was standing right there! But she did not know it was Him.
verses.meaning: Jesus was closer to her than she realised — right behind her. That is the story of our lives too. When we think He is gone, He is standing just behind us.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus is closer than you think.
verses.prayer: Jesus, when I think I can't find You, remind me that You are right beside me. Open my eyes to see You.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever been looking for something and then found it was close to you all along?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus was right there — and Mary didn't recognise Him at first. Are there moments when Jesus has been close to your family and you didn't realise until later?
verses.do_it_today: Look around your day and ask: where has Jesus already been present and I didn't notice?
verses.illustration_prompt: Mary's back to a figure standing in the garden behind her — tall, calm, bathed in morning light, watching her with love. Her face is turned away, not yet knowing who stands there. Golden sunrise filtering through trees. Storybook style.

verses.kjv: Jesus saith unto her, Woman, why weepest thou? whom seekest thou? She, supposing him to be the gardener, saith unto him, Sir, if thou have borne him hence, tell me where thou hast laid him, and I will take him away.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said to her: 'Woman, why are you crying? Who is it you are looking for?' She thought He was the gardener, and said: 'Sir, if you have moved Him, please tell me where you have put Him and I will go and get Him.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus asked, 'Why are you crying? Who are you looking for?' She thought He was the gardener and asked where Jesus was.
verses.meaning: She thought He was the gardener. But the one she was looking for was the one she was talking to. He is always closer than our searching.
verses.memory_phrase: The one you seek is the one beside you.
verses.prayer: Lord Jesus, I am looking for You — and here You are. Open my eyes to see You right where I am.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think Mary didn't recognise Jesus at first, even though she loved Him so much?
verses.family_discussion: Mary was looking for Jesus but couldn't see Him because grief blurred her vision. How do sadness or worry sometimes keep us from seeing Jesus at work?
verses.do_it_today: Ask Jesus today: 'Where are You in this situation I am facing?' Then look for an answer in a verse, a person, or a quiet thought.
verses.illustration_prompt: Mary looking up at a figure she thinks is a gardener — perhaps a hoe or basket nearby, the garden full of morning colour — her face full of earnest, tear-filled hope as she asks her question. Storybook style.

verses.kjv: Jesus saith unto her, Mary. She turned herself, and saith unto him, Rabboni; which is to say, Master.
verses.little_bible: Then Jesus said just one word: 'Mary.' And in that one word — her own name — she knew exactly who He was. She turned and cried out: 'Rabboni!' which means 'Teacher!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said just one word: 'Mary!' And she knew instantly — it was Jesus! She cried, 'Teacher!'
verses.meaning: One word. Her name. That is all it took. The Good Shepherd calls His own sheep by name. Jesus knows YOUR name too — and when He calls it, everything changes.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus knows your name.
verses.prayer: Jesus, You know my name. You know me completely. Thank You that I am not just one of many — I am known by You.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think hearing her own name made Mary immediately know it was Jesus?
verses.family_discussion: The Good Shepherd calls His sheep by name. What does it mean to your family that Jesus personally knows each of you — not as a crowd, but as individuals He loves?
verses.do_it_today: Say your own name out loud and then imagine Jesus saying it. He knows you. He calls you. You are His.
verses.illustration_prompt: Mary spinning around, face transformed from grief to joy in an instant, reaching out toward Jesus who stands in morning light in the garden, His expression full of warmth and love. Tears on her face now full of joy. Storybook style.

verses.kjv: Jesus saith unto her, Touch me not; for I am not yet ascended to my Father: but go to my brethren, and say unto them, I ascend unto my Father, and your Father; and to my God, and your God.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'Do not hold on to Me — I have not yet returned to My Father. But go and tell My brothers: I am going to My Father and your Father, My God and your God.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said, 'Go tell My brothers: I am going to My Father — and your Father too. My God is your God too!'
verses.meaning: Jesus sends Mary as a messenger and gives her extraordinary news: God is now 'your Father' too — because of what Jesus has done, we share His relationship with the Father. This is breathtaking.
verses.memory_phrase: God is your Father too — because of Jesus.
verses.prayer: Father God, thank You that because of Jesus, You are MY Father too. Help me live as Your child.
verses.discussion_question: Jesus says God is 'My Father and your Father.' What does it mean that we can call God our Father because of Jesus?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus made it possible for us to come to God as Father. How does having God as Father change how your family prays?
verses.do_it_today: Start your prayer today with 'Father' — really mean it. You are His child.
verses.illustration_prompt: Mary standing in the garden, face radiant with tears and joy, Jesus gesturing gently toward the city as He sends her. A sense of mission and tenderness in the scene. Morning garden in full colour. Storybook style.

verses.kjv: Mary Magdalene came and told the disciples that she had seen the Lord, and that he had spoken these things unto her.
verses.little_bible: Mary ran to the disciples and told them: 'I have seen the Lord!' And she told them everything He had said to her.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Mary ran to the disciples and said, 'I have seen the Lord!' She told them everything.
verses.meaning: Mary became the first person to announce the resurrection — 'I have seen the Lord!' This was her mission, given by Jesus Himself. The one who stayed and wept was the first to carry the news.
verses.memory_phrase: Those who seek Jesus carry the best news.
verses.prayer: Lord, let me tell people that I have seen You at work in my life. Give me the courage Mary had.
verses.discussion_question: What did Mary tell the disciples? Can you say it in your own words?
verses.family_discussion: Mary was the first to say 'I have seen the Lord!' What is your family's version of that testimony — where have you seen the Lord at work?
verses.do_it_today: Tell one person today: 'I know Jesus is real because...' and finish the sentence from your own experience.
verses.illustration_prompt: Mary in a room full of disciples, face shining with joy and conviction, arms open as she speaks with certainty and excitement. The disciples around her looking astonished and hopeful. Morning light through a window. Storybook style.

verses.kjv: Then the same day at evening, being the first day of the week, when the doors were shut where the disciples were assembled for fear of the Jews, Jesus came and stood in the midst of them, and saith unto them, Peace be unto you.
verses.little_bible: That same evening — the first day of the week — the disciples were gathered in a room with all the doors locked tight, because they were afraid. And then, suddenly, Jesus appeared standing right in the middle of them and said: 'Peace be with you.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: That evening the scared disciples locked the doors. Suddenly Jesus appeared in the room! He said, 'Peace be with you!'
verses.meaning: Locked doors cannot keep Jesus out. Fear cannot prevent His presence. He walks through walls to say 'Peace' to His frightened people. He does the same for us.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus walks through locked doors to bring us peace.
verses.prayer: Jesus, walk through every locked door in my heart today. Come where I am afraid and bring Your peace.
verses.discussion_question: If all the doors were locked, how did Jesus get into the room? What does this tell us about who Jesus is?
verses.family_discussion: The disciples were hiding in fear. Jesus came through the locked door. What 'locked doors' — fears or walls — does your family need Jesus to walk through?
verses.do_it_today: Name one thing you are afraid of. Ask Jesus to walk through that fear and bring His peace.
verses.illustration_prompt: A lantern-lit room with a heavy barred door, disciples huddled fearfully — and Jesus suddenly present in the centre of them, calm and radiant, hands raised in greeting. Astonishment on every face. Storybook style.

verses.kjv: And when he had so said, he shewed unto them his hands and his side. Then were the disciples glad, when they saw the Lord.
verses.little_bible: Then He showed them the wounds in His hands and the wound in His side. And when the disciples saw that it was really the Lord — they were filled with joy!
verses.little_reader_adaptation: He showed them His hands and His side. And the disciples were filled with joy when they saw the Lord!
verses.meaning: The wounds proved it was really Jesus — the same One who died. And in seeing those wounds, fear turned to joy. The scars of the cross are the source of our joy.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus' wounds bring us joy.
verses.prayer: Lord, when I look at what You suffered for me, let my heart fill with joy and gratitude, not just sadness.
verses.discussion_question: Why did the disciples become joyful when they saw the wounds in Jesus' hands and side?
verses.family_discussion: The same thing that proved Jesus was crucified also proved He was risen. How do the wounds of Jesus give you both sorrow for sin and joy for salvation?
verses.do_it_today: Think about what Jesus went through on the cross — and let that fill you with both thankfulness and joy.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus gently turning His hands palm-up to show nail wounds, and lifting His robe slightly to show the wound in His side, while disciples around Him lean forward with expressions moving from fear to pure, tearful joy. Candlelit room. Storybook style.

verses.kjv: Then said Jesus to them again, Peace be unto you: as my Father hath sent me, even so send I you.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said again: 'Peace be with you. Just as My Father sent Me into the world, I am now sending you.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said, 'Peace be with you. As the Father sent Me, I now send you!'
verses.meaning: The mission of Jesus becomes the mission of the disciples — and of every Christian since. We are sent with the same love, the same message, the same authority.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus sends us as the Father sent Him.
verses.prayer: Lord, I receive Your sending. Help me go into the world with Your love and Your message, just as You did.
verses.discussion_question: Jesus was 'sent' by the Father. Now He says He sends His disciples. What does it mean to be sent by Jesus?
verses.family_discussion: Your family is sent — to your street, your school, your workplace. What does being 'sent by Jesus' look like in the everyday places you go?
verses.do_it_today: Go somewhere today — school, work, the shop — knowing that Jesus has sent you there with His love and His peace.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus standing before His disciples, arms extended toward them in a sending gesture, His expression full of authority and love. The disciples standing taller now, no longer fearful, ready to receive their mission. Storybook style.

verses.kjv: And when he had said this, he breathed on them, and saith unto them, Receive ye the Holy Ghost:
verses.little_bible: Then He breathed on them and said: 'Receive the Holy Spirit.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: He breathed on them and said, 'Receive the Holy Spirit!'
verses.meaning: Jesus breathing on them echoes God breathing life into Adam at creation. The new creation begins here. The Spirit is the gift of the risen Jesus to His people.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus gives us His Holy Spirit.
verses.prayer: Jesus, breathe Your Holy Spirit into me. Fill me with Your life and Your power.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think Jesus breathed on them when He gave them the Holy Spirit?
verses.family_discussion: God breathed life into Adam; Jesus breathes the Spirit into His disciples. What does this tell us about what the Holy Spirit does in us?
verses.do_it_today: Take a slow, deep breath and pray: 'Holy Spirit, I receive You. Fill me today.'
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus leaning gently forward, breathing toward the disciples who lean in with open, receptive faces, a soft visible movement of light and breath flowing between them. Intimate and sacred. Storybook style.

verses.kjv: Whose soever sins ye forgive, they are forgiven unto them; and whose soever sins ye retain, they are retained.
verses.little_bible: Then Jesus said: 'If you forgive someone's sins, they are forgiven. If you do not forgive them, they are not forgiven.' He was giving them the ministry of reconciliation — telling people about God's forgiveness.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said, 'When you tell people about God's forgiveness, it really reaches them.'
verses.meaning: The disciples were being sent with the authority to proclaim the forgiveness Jesus won on the cross. This is the ministry of the whole church — bringing people into God's forgiveness.
verses.memory_phrase: We can tell people they are forgiven through Jesus.
verses.prayer: Lord, help me tell people about Your forgiveness. Help me not keep this good news to myself.
verses.discussion_question: Why is it important that the disciples could tell people about God's forgiveness?
verses.family_discussion: We carry the message of forgiveness. Who in your family's world needs to hear that God forgives them through Jesus?
verses.do_it_today: Is there someone you need to forgive today? Or someone you need to tell about God's forgiveness? Take that step.
verses.illustration_prompt: Disciples standing with faces full of new authority and purpose, Jesus before them with His hand raised in commission. Light streams through the room. The sense of a great responsibility being given. Storybook style.

verses.kjv: But Thomas, one of the twelve, called Didymus, was not with them when Jesus came.
verses.little_bible: Thomas — also called Didymus, which means 'the twin' — was one of the twelve disciples, but he had not been there when Jesus appeared.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Thomas, one of the twelve disciples, was not there when Jesus came.
verses.meaning: Thomas missed the evening when Jesus appeared. Sometimes our faith suffers not because we rejected Jesus but because we weren't in the room — we weren't with the gathered community.
verses.memory_phrase: Don't miss out by staying away.
verses.prayer: Lord, help me not miss what You are doing by staying away from Your people. Keep me close to my church family.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever missed something important because you weren't there? How did that feel?
verses.family_discussion: Thomas was absent from the gathering of believers and missed Jesus' appearance. Why is it important to gather with other Christians regularly?
verses.do_it_today: Be present. Don't miss church, small group, or time with your Christian friends — Jesus shows up when His people gather.
verses.illustration_prompt: An empty space at a table where disciples are gathered joyfully — one chair unfilled, a single sandal left under it. The disciples are laughing and energised. Storybook style.

verses.kjv: The other disciples therefore said unto him, We have seen the Lord. But he said unto them, Except I shall see in his hands the print of the nails, and put my finger into the print of the nails, and thrust my hand into his side, I will not believe.
verses.little_bible: The other disciples told Thomas: 'We have seen the Lord!' But Thomas said: 'Unless I see the nail marks in His hands and touch them with my own finger — and put my hand in His side — I will not believe it.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The disciples said, 'We have seen the Lord!' Thomas said, 'I won't believe unless I see and touch His wounds myself!'
verses.meaning: Thomas did not reject Jesus — he set conditions on his faith. He wanted what the others had: personal encounter. And Jesus, graciously, gave him exactly that.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus meets us where our doubt is.
verses.prayer: Lord, I know You are patient with my doubts. Come and meet me where I am, just like You did for Thomas.
verses.discussion_question: Do you think Thomas was wrong to want to see for himself? What do you think about his honesty?
verses.family_discussion: Thomas said out loud what he was thinking. Is your family a place where doubts can be voiced honestly? How does that help faith grow?
verses.do_it_today: If you have a doubt about God, say it out loud — to God and to someone you trust. Honest doubt is the beginning of genuine faith.
verses.illustration_prompt: Thomas standing arms-crossed before the excited disciples, face set in stubborn grief and skepticism, while the other disciples around him gesture and speak earnestly. The contrast between their joy and his pain. Storybook style.

verses.kjv: And after eight days again his disciples were within, and Thomas with them: then came Jesus, the doors being shut, and stood in the midst of them, and said, Peace be unto you.
verses.little_bible: Eight days later, the disciples were gathered again — and this time Thomas was with them. The doors were shut. And again, Jesus appeared and stood in the middle of them and said: 'Peace be with you.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: A week later, they gathered again — Thomas was there this time. Jesus appeared again! He said, 'Peace be with you!'
verses.meaning: Jesus came back. He didn't give up on Thomas. He knew exactly what Thomas had said and He came to meet that specific need. He is patient with every doubter.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus comes back for the one who doubts.
verses.prayer: Thank You, Jesus, that You don't give up on me when I doubt. You come back for me.
verses.discussion_question: Jesus came back a second time just so Thomas could see. What does that tell us about how Jesus feels about Thomas?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus is patient with doubters — He goes looking for them. How does knowing this change the way we treat people who say they don't believe?
verses.do_it_today: Think of someone you know who has doubts about Jesus. Pray for them. Ask God to go to them the way He went to Thomas.
verses.illustration_prompt: The same room as before — now with Thomas included in the circle. Jesus appearing again in the centre, hands raised in greeting, Thomas in the foreground with his eyes wide and mouth open. The same peace, the same open-door moment. Storybook style.

verses.kjv: Then saith he to Thomas, Reach hither thy finger, and behold my hands; and reach hither thy hand, and thrust it into my side: and be not faithless, but believing.
verses.little_bible: Then Jesus turned to Thomas and said: 'Put your finger here — look at My hands. Reach out your hand and put it in My side. Stop doubting and believe!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said to Thomas, 'Touch My hands. Put your hand in My side. Stop doubting and believe!'
verses.meaning: Jesus knew exactly what Thomas had said — word for word — even though Thomas had said it to the other disciples when Jesus wasn't there. Jesus knows our doubts even before we say them to Him.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus knows your doubts — and invites you to believe.
verses.prayer: Jesus, You know my doubts before I even say them. Invite me to believe, the way You invited Thomas.
verses.discussion_question: How did Jesus know exactly what Thomas had said? What does that tell us about Jesus?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus didn't rebuke Thomas in front of everyone — He addressed him personally and specifically. How can we do the same for people struggling with faith?
verses.do_it_today: Tell Jesus your biggest doubt today — out loud. Then listen for His invitation to believe.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus facing Thomas directly, hand extended palm-up showing the nail mark, the other hand gently holding Thomas's wrist and guiding it forward. Thomas's face full of awe and breaking open. Intimate and tender. Storybook style.

verses.kjv: And Thomas answered and said unto him, My Lord and my God.
verses.little_bible: Thomas answered — and all his doubts fell away in one moment. He said: 'My Lord and my God!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Thomas said, 'My Lord and my God!' It was the most important thing he ever said.
verses.meaning: Thomas's confession — 'My Lord and my God' — is the highest confession of faith in the entire Gospel of John. The doubter became the greatest confessor. God wastes nothing — not even our doubts.
verses.memory_phrase: 'My Lord and my God!' — the greatest thing we can ever say.
verses.prayer: My Lord and my God! Jesus, You are everything. I believe in You with all that I am.
verses.discussion_question: Thomas said two things: 'My Lord' and 'My God.' What do you think each of those means?
verses.family_discussion: The biggest doubter in the room made the biggest confession of faith. How does Thomas give hope to people who struggle to believe?
verses.do_it_today: Say it out loud: 'My Lord and my God!' Say it slowly. Mean it.
verses.illustration_prompt: Thomas on his knees before Jesus, face streaming with tears of wonder and worship, both hands reaching up toward Jesus who looks at him with profound love. The other disciples watching in hushed awe. Candlelight and radiance. Storybook style.

verses.kjv: Jesus saith unto him, Thomas, because thou hast seen me, thou hast believed: blessed are they that have not seen, and yet have believed.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said to Thomas: 'You believe because you have seen Me. But how wonderfully blessed are all the people who believe in Me without seeing Me — and still trust Me completely!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said, 'You believed because you saw. But how blessed are those who believe without seeing!'
verses.meaning: This blessing is for YOU — for everyone who has not physically seen the risen Jesus but still believes. You are the blessed ones Jesus is talking about.
verses.memory_phrase: Blessed are those who believe without seeing.
verses.prayer: Jesus, I have not seen You with my eyes — but I believe in You. Thank You for calling me blessed.
verses.discussion_question: Jesus says YOU are blessed for believing without seeing. How does that make you feel?
verses.family_discussion: Every person who has believed in Jesus since the first century has done so without seeing Him physically. You are part of a great chain of blessed believers. Talk about that blessing together.
verses.do_it_today: Write down: 'I am blessed because I believe in Jesus without seeing Him.' Keep it somewhere you will see it.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus speaking directly, eyes full of warmth — and beyond the room, a sense of light streaming outward toward people in all times and places who believe. A visual of blessing going out to millions across the ages. Storybook style.

verses.kjv: And many other signs truly did Jesus in the presence of his disciples, which are not written in this book:
verses.little_bible: Jesus did many other miracles and signs in front of His disciples — more than could ever be written down in one book.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus did many more miracles — too many to write in this book!
verses.meaning: The Gospel of John is not the whole story of Jesus — it is a carefully chosen selection designed for a purpose. More happened than we know.
verses.memory_phrase: There is more to Jesus than any book can hold.
verses.prayer: Lord Jesus, You are greater than I can fully know or understand. Help me keep discovering more of You.
verses.discussion_question: Can you imagine all the things Jesus did that weren't written down? What do you wish you could know?
verses.family_discussion: We have enough in Scripture to believe and be saved — but Jesus is inexhaustibly greater. How does knowing there is always more to discover about Jesus excite you?
verses.do_it_today: Ask a question about Jesus you have never asked before. Look it up or ask someone. There is always more to discover.
verses.illustration_prompt: An open book in the foreground with words glowing on the page, and behind it a vast, bright landscape full of scenes and stories too numerous to see — miracles and moments beyond counting. Storybook style.

verses.kjv: But these are written, that ye might believe that Jesus is the Christ, the Son of God; and that believing ye might have life through his name.
verses.little_bible: But these stories and miracles were written down for one reason: so that you will believe that Jesus is the Christ — the Son of God — and so that by believing in Him, you will have life through His name.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: These stories were written so you will believe Jesus is God's Son — and have life in His name!
verses.meaning: John's purpose statement is the purpose of the entire Bible for every reader: believe that Jesus is the Christ, the Son of God, and have life. That life is for you, right now, in His name.
verses.memory_phrase: Believe in Jesus — and have life.
verses.prayer: Jesus, I believe You are the Christ, the Son of God. And I receive the life You give. Thank You.
verses.discussion_question: John says this book was written specifically so that YOU might believe. How does it feel to know the Bible was written with you in mind?
verses.family_discussion: The whole Bible has a purpose: to lead you to believe in Jesus and have life. Has your family responded to that invitation? Talk about it today.
verses.do_it_today: Say out loud: 'I believe that Jesus is the Christ, the Son of God. I have life through His name.' Then live like it is true — because it is.
verses.illustration_prompt: An open Bible glowing with golden light, the words on the page seeming to float up and out toward the reader, a sense of invitation and life. A child's hands reaching to receive what comes from the page. Storybook style.
